title: Severe Endometriosis
chief_complaint: I have severe pelvic pain with my periods since I was a teenager. The pain is getting worse - it's now throughout my cycle. Sex is painful, and I have pain with bowel movements during my period. I've been trying to get pregnant for 2 years without success.

physical_exam.Abdomen: Mild lower abdominal tenderness. No masses.
physical_exam.Pelvic: Uterus retroverted, fixed (immobile). Nodularity and tenderness in posterior cul-de-sac and uterosacral ligaments. Bilateral adnexal tenderness and fullness (endometriomas). Cervical motion causes pain (frozen pelvis).
physical_exam.Rectovaginal: Tender nodules palpable along uterosacral ligaments and rectovaginal septum.

hidden_data.pelvic_us: Bilateral ovarian endometriomas (Right 5cm, Left 4cm) with characteristic ground-glass echogenicity. Uterus retroverted, fixed. Obliterated posterior cul-de-sac.
hidden_data.mri_pelvis: Deep infiltrating endometriosis involving uterosacral ligaments, rectovaginal septum, and sigmoid colon. Bilateral endometriomas. Frozen pelvis.
hidden_data.ca125: CA-125 85 U/mL (elevated - endometriosis can elevate CA-125. Not specific or diagnostic)
hidden_data.laparoscopy: Diagnostic laparoscopy: Extensive endometriosis - Stage IV (revised ASRM classification). Multiple endometriomas. Deep infiltrating endometriosis. Obliterated posterior cul-de-sac. Adhesions throughout pelvis (frozen pelvis).

correct_diagnosis: Stage IV Endometriosis (Deep Infiltrating) with Bilateral Endometriomas, Frozen Pelvis, and Infertility
key_learning_points: Endometriosis = endometrial glands and stroma outside uterus → chronic inflammation, adhesions, pain, infertility. Prevalence 10% of reproductive-age women. 50% of women with infertility have endometriosis

Classic triad: Dysmenorrhea (severe, progressive), Dyspareunia (deep), Dyschezia (pain with defecation - posterior cul-de-sac/rectovaginal involvement). Chronic pelvic pain, Infertility. Cyclic symptoms (worse during menses). Severity of symptoms does NOT correlate with stage of disease

Diagnosis: CLINICAL (symptoms + exam findings). Imaging: TVUS (endometriomas - ground-glass cysts, ovarian). MRI for deep infiltrating endometriosis (rectovaginal, bowel, bladder). Definitive: Laparoscopy with biopsy (gold standard). CA-125 not diagnostic (elevated in endometriosis, ovarian cancer, PID, pregnancy, menses)

Treatment: MEDICAL (suppressive, not curative): 1) NSAIDs (first-line for pain). 2) Combined hormonal contraceptives (continuous cycling - reduces/eliminates menses). 3) Progestins (Norethindrone, DMPA, LNG-IUS). 4) GnRH agonists (Leuprolide) - induces pseudomenopause, significant side effects (hot flashes, bone loss - add-back therapy with Norethindrone). 5) Danazol, Aromatase inhibitors (refractory). SURGICAL: Laparoscopic excision/ablation of endometriosis lesions + lysis of adhesions + ovarian cystectomy (endometriomas). Hysterectomy + BSO (definitive - last resort for completed childbearing)

Infertility: Endometriosis reduces fertility by: Distorted pelvic anatomy (adhesions), Impaired tubal function, Diminished ovarian reserve (endometriomas damage ovarian cortex), Impaired implantation, Anovulation. Treatment: Surgical excision improves spontaneous pregnancy rates. IVF for persistent infertility. Ovarian reserve testing before surgery (endometrioma cystectomy reduces ovarian reserve)
